name: Tharsis, Ermita de la Virgen de la Peña, Puebla de Guzmán – ruta circular
region_intro: Mountain biking around Alosno offers routes through a landscape characterized by small hills and varied topography, influenced by its historical mining past. The region features undulating terrain with historical tracks and paths, surrounded by trees and diverse flora. These natural settings provide opportunities for exploring both challenging and more relaxed cycling routes. The area's elevation changes and natural features make it suitable for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Alosno The most popular mountain bike trail is Tharsis, Ermita de la Virgen de la Peña, Puebla de Guzmán – ruta circular, a 22.3 miles (35.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 58 minutes to complete. This route passes by the historic Tharsis mining area and the scenic Hermitage of Our Lady of La Peña. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Peña Maya loop from Cabezo de Odma, a moderate 21.6 miles (34.7 km) path. This route explores the local hills and natural surroundings, offering varied terrain. Local mountain bikers also love the Molino Zahurdón loop from Villanueva de los Castillejos, a 13.3 miles (21.4 km) trail leading through wooded areas and open landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 41 minutes. Mountain biking around Alosno is defined by small hills, historical mining areas, and quiet country roads. The network offers options for different ability levels, including easy and moderate routes. The routes in Alosno are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 100 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Alosno's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the difficulty level of mountain bike trails around Alosno?
A: The mountain bike trails around Alosno cater to various skill levels. You'll find a mix of routes, with 1 easy trail and 8 moderate trails. There are no difficult trails listed, making the area suitable for most riders looking for enjoyable experiences.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Alosno?
A: There are 9 mountain bike trails available around Alosno. These routes offer diverse experiences through the region's unique landscape.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the mountain bike trails around Alosno?
A: The terrain around Alosno is characterized by small hills and varied topography, influenced by its historical mining past. You'll encounter undulating landscapes with historical tracks and paths, surrounded by trees and diverse flora. Many routes also incorporate quiet country roads, offering a mix of natural and semi-paved surfaces.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike trails in Alosno?
A: Yes, there is at least one easy mountain bike trail around Alosno, which is suitable for families or those seeking a more relaxed ride. The region's quiet country roads and less challenging sections make it possible to find enjoyable routes for various ages and skill levels.

Q: What interesting sights can I see while mountain biking near Alosno?
A: While mountain biking near Alosno, you can explore several interesting landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995556906/tharsis-ermita-de-la-virgen-de-la-pena-puebla-de-guzman-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tharsis, Ermita de la Virgen de la Peña, Puebla de Guzmán – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt; route passes by the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334987" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tharsis Open-Pit Mine&lt;/a&gt; and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5333422" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of La Peña of Puebla de Guzmán&lt;/a&gt;. Another route, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38159621" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tharsis Open-Pit Mine loop from Alosno&lt;/a&gt;, also takes you through the mining area, offering a glimpse into the region's industrial past amidst natural surroundings.

Q: Are there many loop mountain bike trails available?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Alosno are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995556906/tharsis-ermita-de-la-virgen-de-la-pena-puebla-de-guzman-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tharsis, Ermita de la Virgen de la Peña, Puebla de Guzmán – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24522633" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Maya loop from Cabezo de Odma&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844211" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino Zahurdón loop from Villanueva de los Castillejos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Alosno?
A: The mountain bike routes in Alosno are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from 2 reviews. Over 100 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, suggesting a positive and engaging experience for those who visit.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Alosno?
A: Given Alosno's location in Andalusia, the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months generally offer the most pleasant weather for mountain biking, with milder temperatures. Summers can be very hot, while winters are typically mild but can have some rain.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on mountain bike trails in Alosno are not detailed, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in natural areas, to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always check local signage for any specific restrictions.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink near the mountain bike trails?
A: Alosno, being a town, offers various amenities including cafes, pubs, and restaurants where you can refuel after your ride. While not directly on every trailhead, these establishments are typically accessible within the town or nearby villages.

Q: What are the typical lengths and durations of mountain bike routes in Alosno?
A: The mountain bike routes around Alosno vary in length and duration. You can find trails ranging from approximately 13.3 miles (21.4 km), which might take around 1 hour 41 minutes, to longer routes like 22.3 miles (35.8 km) taking nearly 3 hours, or even 28.4 miles (45.7 km) which could take over 3 hours. This variety allows you to choose a route that fits your available time and fitness level.
tips: This village has been a mining area for many centuries, in whose mines, above all, massive pyrite was extracted for the manufacture of sulfuric acid.

The Hermitage is located on the so-called Cerro del Águila, about four kilometers from Puebla de Guzmán. It was probably built in the 16th century, although the most important construction works were undertaken in the 17th century.

The Church of the Holy Cross in Puebla de Guzmán (province of Huelva, Andalusia) is a temple of great historical, artistic, and spiritual value.

Original construction: Late 16th and early 17th centuries.
It was praised in 1645 by Francisco de Rada.
Between 1748 and 1757, significant renovations were carried out, including a new main chapel whose roof was converted into a watchtower.
It suffered severe damage during a fire in July 1936, at the beginning of the Spanish Civil War. It was rebuilt by the architect José María Pérez Carasa.
